Content: Ray: It'll be all right, Tim.
Content_zh: Ray：沒事的，Tim。

Content: Hansen: It's all right. We'll figure this out.
Content_zh: Hansen：沒事的，我們會想辦法。

Content: Tim: Are you saying I shouldn't do it?
Content_zh: Tim：你是說我不該這麼做嗎？

Content: Hansen: Don't do it. Don't do it.
Content_zh: Hansen：不要，不要。

Content: Ray: We'll make it work.
Content_zh: Ray：我們會讓它行得通。

Content: Tim: You're just mad because if I did this it would not solve the problem for you guys.
Content_zh: Tim：你只是氣我這麼做，也解決不了你們的問題。

Content: Hansen: It just makes it worse actually. Ray: Yeah.
Content_zh: Hansen：其實只會讓事情更糟。Ray：對。

Content: Hansen: That's right. It makes it easier for you.
Content_zh: Hansen：沒錯。只是讓你比較輕鬆。

Content: Ray: There're still pickup lines that haven't been written, aren't there?
Content_zh: Ray：還有一些補錄台詞沒寫，對吧？

Content: [Music] Tim: I really am trying everything to make sure the game comes out this year.
Content_zh: [音樂] Tim：我真的什麼方法都試了，就是想確保遊戲今年能推出。

Content: Ray: December. What are we in, October, November?
Content_zh: Ray：十二月。我們現在是十月還十一月？

Content: Yeah, I know that's not gonna happen.
Content_zh: 對，我知道那不可能。

Content: Tim: I gotta finish.
Content_zh: Tim：我得把它寫完。

Content: I'm so close to being done with the dialogue for the game which would be a huge milestone in my life
Content_zh: 我快寫完遊戲對白了，這會是我人生的一大里程碑，

Content: because all I can think about is the dialogue for this game.
Content_zh: 因為我滿腦子都只有這款遊戲的對白。

Content: There's a lot of dialogue in this game.
Content_zh: 這遊戲的對白真的很多。

Content: Okay, we're gonna start.
Content_zh: 好，我們要開始了。

Content: Weeklies, here we are, back on the project.
Content_zh: 週會，我們又回到這個專案了。

Content: Hey, guess what. I finished all the dialogue for the game.
Content_zh: 嘿，你們猜怎麼了。我把遊戲的所有對白都寫完了。

Content: It's awesome. Yay!
Content_zh: 太棒了。耶！

Content: [Clapping] That's a relief.
Content_zh: [掌聲] 終於鬆了一口氣。

Content: Crook: Finally.
Content_zh: Crook：終於。

Content: Schafer: Well, I mean you didn't have to put it that way, but yeah, yeah, finally.
Content_zh: Schafer：呃，你也不用講成那樣，不過對，對，終於。

Content: Crook: Someone had to say it!
Content_zh: Crook：總得有人說出口！

Content: I know I was going on my calendar today and there's a big recurring appointment for Reds writing and I went and I deleted it.
Content_zh: 我今天看行事曆，上面有一個 Red’s 寫作的定期行程，然後我就把它刪掉了。

Content: Oh, my God, this is exciting.
Content_zh: 天啊，這太令人興奮了。

Content: I'm never gonna write again.
Content_zh: 我再也不寫東西了。

Content: Never write again. Never write again.
Content_zh: 再也不寫了。再也不寫了。

Content: So actually I'm playing through the game right now and I'm making notes of what dialogue I need to write.
Content_zh: 其實我現在正在跑一遍遊戲，順便記下還需要寫哪些對白。

Content: Other than that I'm totally done.
Content_zh: 除此之外我就真的全寫完了。

Content: [Music] And then Tim nobly finished the last of the dialogue
Content_zh: [音樂] 然後 Tim 英勇地完成了最後的對白，

Content: and everyone was happy forever, the end.
Content_zh: 大家從此永遠幸福快樂，完。

Content: And that's the end of that chapter.
Content_zh: 這一章就到此結束。

Content: Does anybody have any questions?
Content_zh: 有人有問題嗎？

Content: Broken Age, broken Age.
Content_zh: 《Broken Age》，《Broken Age》。

Content: That's our rousing chant, broken age.
Content_zh: 這就是我們振奮人心的口號，《Broken Age》。

Content: Bye.
Content_zh: 拜。

Content: Peck: What's new, man?
Content_zh: Peck：最近怎樣？

Content: We have gotten a butt ton of writing from Tim.
Content_zh: Tim 給了我們超多新寫好的內容。

Content: Crook: It's really good writing.
Content_zh: Crook：寫得真的很好。

Content: And the performances that we've gotten back are really good and so that kind of puts the pressure on you
Content_zh: 而且我們拿回來的配音表演也很棒，所以這會讓你有點壓力，

Content: as an animator that you want to do that writing and those performances justice.
Content_zh: 身為動畫師，你會想把那些文字和表演好好呈現出來。

Content: So that's, that's pressure, but good pressure.
Content_zh: 所以那是壓力，但是好的壓力。

Content: It feels like things are moving smoothly.
Content_zh: 感覺事情進展得很順。

Content: It feels that way.
Content_zh: 感覺是這樣。

Content: Anna: We're like finishing in record time.
Content_zh: Anna：我們好像用破紀錄的速度在收尾。

Content: We're actually ahead of schedule in a lot of ways which is like, you know, pretty rare for us because usually we're pretty slammed.
Content_zh: 我們其實很多地方都超前進度，這對我們來說很少見，因為通常都忙到爆。

Content: Lee: Yes, I can show you some mockups that we're working on in Vella, yeah, yeah.
Content_zh: Lee：對，我可以給你看一些我們在做的 Vella 段落 mockup，對。

Content: This scene here is where Vella starts.
Content_zh: 這個場景是 Vella 一開始所在的地方。

Content: Players have already seen this, you know, the split screen at the very end of the game, and this is kind of what that scene continues as.
Content_zh: 玩家已經看過這裡，就是遊戲最後的分割畫面，而這大概就是那場戲接下來的樣子。

Content: And then this is the first puzzle you see here is her skill here.
Content_zh: 然後這裡看到的是第一個謎題，也就是她在這裡要做的事。

Content: So this is a fairly elaborate puzzle that the player has to hit, and so a lot of this is just breakdowns of how it all works.
Content_zh: 這是玩家會碰到的相當複雜謎題，所以這些大多是在拆解它的運作方式。

Content: So we have the Hex Pals are returning.
Content_zh: 所以 Hexipal 會回來。

Content: Of course. I love the Hex Pals so I'm hoping to do more with them this time.
Content_zh: 當然。我很喜歡 Hexipal，所以希望這次能多用一點。

Content: I think the Hex Pals and the Spoon, that stuff was all the big hits, like the surprise, like everyone loved these little side characters.
Content_zh: 我覺得 Hexipal 和湯匙那些東西都很受歡迎，算是驚喜，大家都很愛這些小配角。

Content: We have now heard a lot more feedback on that first act than you ever would if you were just gonna release it at once
Content_zh: 我們現在收到的第一幕回饋，比一次推出整款遊戲時能聽到的多得多，

Content: because it's been out in the wild and people have lived with it.
Content_zh: 因為它已經放到玩家手上，大家也跟它相處了一段時間。

Content: We've lived it for a while so we have more perspective on it.
Content_zh: 我們自己也陪它走了一陣子，所以更有判斷角度。

Content: Maybe that means the choices we're making for Act 2 are just a little bit better formed than they would've been if it was just,
Content_zh: 也許這代表我們為第二幕做的選擇會更成熟一點，

Content: you know, one big push.
Content_zh: 比起原本一口氣做完來得更好。

Content: And then just a lot of scenes in Act 2, I can walk you around some of those if you want to see.
Content_zh: 第二幕還有很多場景，如果你想看，我可以帶你看幾個。

Content: I can show you some with the spaceship stuff.
Content_zh: 我可以給你看一些太空船相關的東西。

Content: So we've tried to go through and make it look like you're driving by the countryside and damage some of the rooms.
Content_zh: 所以我們試著讓它看起來像一路掠過鄉間，並且把一些房間做成受損狀態。

Content: Schafer: Exciting, really going places.
Content_zh: Schafer：真刺激，真的要遠走高飛了。

Content: Petty: If you go in that doorway on the left there's some ambience there.
Content_zh: Petty：如果你進左邊那個門口，那裡有一些環境音。

Content: Schafer: It's too bad we have puzzles in both those places because it'd be funny to see a room that was there before and then just isn't there,
Content_zh: Schafer：可惜那兩個地方都有謎題，不然看到原本在那裡的房間突然不見了會很好笑，

Content: like so the door is open like to the avalanche area.
Content_zh: 像是門一打開就通往雪崩區。

Content: No, we need that one.
Content_zh: 不行，那個我們還需要。

Content: Or to the like the friendship circle where something just opens and then it just like [explosive noise] just space in there.
Content_zh: 或是通往友誼圈，某個東西一打開，然後就像 [爆炸聲] 裡面只剩太空。

Content: It could just be you open it and it's just the shards of a room and there's just a bunch of yarn pals hanging on.
Content_zh: 也可以是你打開之後，只看到房間碎片，還有一堆毛線夥伴掛在那裡。

Content: Aaahh!
Content_zh: 啊啊啊！

Content: They're just stuck out there.
Content_zh: 他們就困在外面。

Content: Petty: I was thinking that for any of the rooms like that, that we should have the doors and then continue on in animation state
Content_zh: Petty：我在想，像那樣的房間，我們應該讓門維持某個動畫狀態，

Content: where they can't quite close or open.
Content_zh: 就是關不太起來，也打不太開。

Content: They're just kinda...
Content_zh: 它們就有點……

Content: Schafer: They're closing over a severed human foot.
Content_zh: Schafer：它們正在關上，夾住一隻被砍斷的人腳。

Content: Peck: There's some cool game play that's like nonstandard stuff where like you're floating in zero gravity
Content_zh: Peck：有一些很酷、比較不典型的玩法，像是你在零重力裡漂浮，

Content: and so like you play around with the physics of that.
Content_zh: 然後利用那種物理效果來玩。

Content: So this is one of those instances where we were in a brainstorm meeting and we were like what if the whole ship just like rotated
Content_zh: 這就是那種例子，我們在腦力激盪會議裡說，如果整艘船就這樣旋轉，

Content: because it's taking off, right?
Content_zh: 因為它正在起飛，對吧？

Content: And everyone was like, oh shit.
Content_zh: 然後大家都說，喔，靠。

Content: Offscreen: Once you solve that and you get up here and the doors open, that's when we do the;
Content_zh: 畫外音：你解開之後到了這裡，門一打開，我們就在那時候做那個……

Content: and then we cut to the old scene and now you're floating above it.
Content_zh: 然後切回舊場景，現在你漂在它上方。

Content: You're like, how do I get down there?
Content_zh: 你會想，我要怎麼下去？

Content: Schafer: So she could be spinning.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以她可以一直旋轉。

Content: So there could be a timing thing that you have to do a certain thing which is aim a certain direction.
Content_zh: 所以可以有個時機謎題，你得在特定時候做某件事，也就是瞄準某個方向。

Content: Petty: We could have like the boy's magnetic boots stuck on the hole, you know, if we wanted to use those somehow.
Content_zh: Petty：如果我們想用那些東西，可以讓男孩的磁力靴卡在洞口之類的。

Content: Schafer: And you just like want to drift over to it and grab it and use it to like.
Content_zh: Schafer：然後你會想飄過去抓住它，用它來……

Content: Petty: Oh, wait, there's a scarf on the suit.
Content_zh: Petty：喔，等等，那套太空裝上有條圍巾。

Content: So if you could take the scarf off the floating suit.
Content_zh: 所以如果你能把漂浮太空裝上的圍巾拿下來……

Content: Peck: Not exactly your typical adventure game.
Content_zh: Peck：這可不是典型的冒險遊戲。

Content: Walk to "A" to "B".
Content_zh: 從「A」走到「B」就好。

Content: Crap, I missed it. It's a timing puzzle.
Content_zh: 糟糕，我錯過了。這是時機謎題。

Content: You have to like line up the rotations of these two things.
Content_zh: 你得把這兩個東西的旋轉時機對上。

Content: Damn it. I missed it again.
Content_zh: 可惡，我又錯過了。

Content: I anticipate a lot of amazing play testing feedback from this with people being like fuck that mini game.
Content_zh: 我預期試玩測試會給我們很多精彩回饋，大家會說去他的那個小遊戲。

Content: We've also had to think outside the box in terms of like, especially because we're reusing environments so we can't just make
Content_zh: 我們也得跳脫框架思考，尤其因為我們在重複使用環境，所以不能直接做

Content: a new environment that supports really difficult puzzles.
Content_zh: 一個能支撐高難度謎題的新環境。

Content: We have to use the environments we've already made.
Content_zh: 我們得使用已經做好的環境。

Content: And so yeah, we consciously wanted to try and, try and do things that we hadn't done before.
Content_zh: 所以對，我們是有意識地想試著做一些以前沒做過的事。

Content: Let's have a puzzle that goes across multiple scenes.
Content_zh: 來做一個橫跨多個場景的謎題吧。

Content: Let's like rotate the ship.
Content_zh: 來讓船旋轉吧。

Content: Let's play with the fact that we're on a spaceship.
Content_zh: 來利用我們在太空船上的設定吧。

Content: We're in space.
Content_zh: 我們在太空裡。

Content: Maybe there's no gravity.
Content_zh: 也許沒有重力。

Content: There's this really weird plot device that we have in Act 1 where like the mom could magically manipulate everything on the ship.
Content_zh: 第一幕裡有個很奇怪的劇情設定，就是媽媽可以神奇地操控船上的一切。

Content: What if Vella could gain access to that system?
Content_zh: 如果 Vella 能取得那個系統的權限呢？

Content: Like, that's a really cool idea to pursue.
Content_zh: 那真的是個很酷、值得發展的點子。

Content: And like a lot of really great puzzle ideas came out of that kind of, that kind of thinking.
Content_zh: 很多很棒的謎題點子，就是從那種思考方式裡冒出來的。

Content: Kipnis: It's just like ways to interact with the ship or to see the same space in a completely original context.
Content_zh: Kipnis：就是用不同方式跟船互動，或是在全新的脈絡下看同一個空間。

Content: Schafer: And the team is starting to wire out the first few puzzles for Vella on the spaceship now.
Content_zh: Schafer：團隊現在開始替 Vella 在太空船上的前幾個謎題搭出雛形。

Content: That's mostly what was going on.
Content_zh: 大概就是這些。

Content: Reds. Unless I forgot something.
Content_zh: Red’s 的部分。除非我漏了什麼。

Content: Anyone want to do anything else?
Content_zh: 還有人想補充什麼嗎？

Content: Okay, now I have to go the bad and not fun part of the meeting where I have some bad news to tell you about,
Content_zh: 好，現在要進入會議中很糟、也不好玩的部分了，我有壞消息要告訴大家，

Content: someone else leaving the company which is never very fun.
Content_zh: 又有人要離開公司，這從來都不好受。

Content: Isa is leaving and oh, man, come on up here, Isa.
Content_zh: Isa 要離開了，唉，Isa，來前面吧。

Content: Stamos: So I just want to let you know I'll be cheering Double Fine on, and I've loved my time here.
Content_zh: Stamos：我只是想讓大家知道，我會繼續替 Double Fine 加油，我也很喜歡在這裡的時光。

Content: [Applause] Schafer: Luckily we have Matt Hanson who was our right-hand man,
Content_zh: [掌聲] Schafer：幸好我們有 Matt Hanson，他原本就是我們的左右手，

Content: and he's stepping right into the role of being the VP of product development so he's doing that job which means he probably has less time
Content_zh: 他會直接接下產品開發副總的角色，所以他要做那份工作，也代表他可能比較沒時間

Content: to do producer type stuff on Broken Age.
Content_zh: 處理《Broken Age》的製作人相關事務。

Content: Yeah, I might technically be short a producer.
Content_zh: 對，嚴格來說我可能少了一位製作人。

Content: I hadn't thought about that. Now I'm terrified.
Content_zh: 我之前沒想到這點。現在我嚇壞了。

Content: I'm never gonna make December. We'll see.
Content_zh: 我一定趕不上十二月了。再看看吧。

Content: That'll delay them in making that schedule that says we can't make December so that way I can hopefully get the game done before anyone realizes it's impossible.
Content_zh: 這會拖慢他們做出那份「我們趕不上十二月」的時程表，這樣我就有希望在大家發現不可能之前把遊戲做完。

Content: I think we're gonna make December.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們會趕上十二月。

Content: We'll never make December.
Content_zh: 我們絕對趕不上十二月。

Content: Shut up. Shut up.
Content_zh: 閉嘴。閉嘴。

Content: Ow, that really hurts.
Content_zh: 噢，真的很痛。

Content: Peck: That's like above my pay grade, dude.
Content_zh: Peck：老兄，那已經超出我的職權範圍了。

Content: Russell: Like, what do I think?
Content_zh: Russell：你是說我怎麼想？

Content: Just for me or the whole game?
Content_zh: 只問我這部分，還是整款遊戲？

Content: Offscreen: The whole game I guess.
Content_zh: 畫外音：我想是整款遊戲。

Content: Malena and I think it's gonna be March.
Content_zh: Malena 和我覺得會是三月。

Content: I'm not sure who thought ever December was really gonna be it.
Content_zh: 我不太確定到底有誰真的以為會是十二月。

Content: Offscreen: Tim. Russell: Yeah.
Content_zh: 畫外音：Tim。Russell：對。

Content: Kipnis: Yeah, I don't, I don't actually know the release date at this point.
Content_zh: Kipnis：對，我現在其實不知道發行日是什麼時候。

Content: I think it's gonna be largely driven by how long it takes for all of the animations to be final
Content_zh: 我覺得很大程度會取決於所有動畫要多久才能完成定稿，

Content: because the animators are gonna be slammed pretty hard since some of the voice is getting into the game so late unfortunately.
Content_zh: 因為有些配音很晚才進到遊戲裡，動畫師們恐怕會被操得很慘。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, some of the VO talent has been harder to get in the studio than others,
Content_zh: Schafer：對，有些配音演員比其他人更難約進錄音室，

Content: and so you have a couple big characters that have yet to be recorded and that holds up all the cut scenes.
Content_zh: 所以有幾個重要角色還沒錄音，這會卡住所有過場動畫。

Content: We're looking at cut scene layouts together so that Ray can know whether they're ready to go.
Content_zh: 我們一起看過場動畫的 layout，讓 Ray 知道它們是不是可以進下一步。

Content: What's a layout, Ray?
Content_zh: Ray，layout 是什麼？

Content: Crook: A layout is a first pass of the scene with the characters all in their sort of place and with the dialogue timed out.
Content_zh: Crook：layout 是場景的第一版，角色大致就位，對白時間也先抓好了。

Content: Schafer: The rough cameras, the timing, but none of the animation.
Content_zh: Schafer：有粗略鏡位和時間點，但還沒有動畫。

Content: Crook: Yeah, none of the animation.
Content_zh: Crook：對，還沒有動畫。

Content: Schafer: Whipped Cream Hill fail.
Content_zh: Schafer：鮮奶油山失敗版。

Content: Crook: She gets it too.
Content_zh: Crook：她也中招了。

Content: Schafer: Who did that one?
Content_zh: Schafer：那個是誰做的？

Content: Crook: Miyuki.
Content_zh: Crook：Miyuki。

Content: Schafer: Is it the same?
Content_zh: Schafer：是一樣的嗎？

Content: Crook: It's the exact same rig, yeah.
Content_zh: Crook：對，是完全一樣的 rig。

Content: I think it's funny that it's exactly the same.
Content_zh: 我覺得完全一樣反而很好笑。

Content: Schafer: If we can just repeat the same, she can have a whole different; I can't even think of a word.
Content_zh: Schafer：如果我們能直接重複同一個，她就可以有完全不同的……我連詞都想不出來。

Content: Crook: I think the joke's funny because it is the same. Schafer: Mm-hmm.
Content_zh: Crook：我覺得笑點就是它一模一樣。Schafer：嗯哼。

Content: Crook: If you want something different.
Content_zh: Crook：如果你想要不一樣的話。

Content: Schafer: No, it's good.
Content_zh: Schafer：不用，這樣很好。

Content: And then we're just finishing up on the Alpha and the G-2 act and then we've got to do the finale.
Content_zh: 然後我們正在收尾 Alpha 和 G-2 這段，接著還得做結局。

Content: Petty: It's a whole area, new characters.
Content_zh: Petty：那是一整個區域，還有新角色。

Content: It's probably a lot of cut scene work.
Content_zh: 可能會有很多過場動畫工作。

Content: It's probably a ton of animation.
Content_zh: 動畫量大概會很多。

Content: When it's not really a 3-D world and you have to stage things specifically and you don't want to paint a million scenes,
Content_zh: 當它不是真正的 3D 世界，而你得特別安排場面，又不想畫一百萬個場景時，

Content: a lot of it really comes down to exactly what is going on in the writing and the puzzles.
Content_zh: 很多事情其實都取決於劇本和謎題裡到底發生了什麼。

Content: Schafer: Even though we haven't been storyboarding a lot in the process, like this would be an important one to do
Content_zh: Schafer：雖然我們在這個流程裡沒有大量畫分鏡，但這段會很需要做，

Content: because I know Peter's already talking about musical score and that London, I mean that Melbourne Symphony Orchestra gig is gonna come up.
Content_zh: 因為我知道 Peter 已經在談配樂了，而且倫敦，我是說墨爾本交響樂團那場錄音也快到了。

Content: And I think this would be a great place to have special music and not use existing cues and stuff.
Content_zh: 我覺得這會很適合做特別配樂，而不是用現有的音樂 cue。

Content: So hopefully we can actually storyboard it, timing it out in the animatic and commit to that, lock that,
Content_zh: 所以希望我們真的能替它畫分鏡，在 animatic 裡把時間抓好，然後確定下來、鎖定，

Content: and then have them build a score to that while we're making it.
Content_zh: 再讓他們在我們製作時依照那個寫配樂。

Content: Do you want to go shot by shot then?
Content_zh: 那你想逐鏡頭看嗎？

Content: Okay, so that's the opening shot, just like here's the situation.
Content_zh: 好，這是開場鏡頭，就是交代目前狀況。

Content: It's happened.
Content_zh: 事情已經發生了。

Content: And then we cut into an interior shot and I was thinking interior because I was hoping we'd just fake it and it shows a bunch of red smoke because
Content_zh: 然後我們切到室內鏡頭，我想用室內是因為希望可以偷吃步，只顯示一堆紅煙，因為

Content: like they're in the room that's the hottest so it's like smoke is coming up.
Content_zh: 他們在最熱的那個房間裡，所以煙一直冒上來。

Content: Crook: So yeah, so this is the animatic with Lee's storyboards.
Content_zh: Crook：對，所以這是用 Lee 分鏡做成的 animatic。

Content: It's very actiony, very dramatic.
Content_zh: 動作感很強，也很戲劇化。

Content: It'll require some really good performance from whoever ends up animating it.
Content_zh: 最後負責動畫的人得做出很好的表演。

Content: To really just sit down and have a big, juicy acting scene, for me that's, I get the most enjoyment out of that
Content_zh: 能真正坐下來做一場很飽滿的表演戲，對我來說，那是我最享受的部分，

Content: because that's when your personality can come out in a way.
Content_zh: 因為那時候你的個性某種程度上可以展現出來。

Content: Yeah, it's gonna be a lot of work.
Content_zh: 對，這工作量會很大。

Content: It should be.
Content_zh: 本來就該這樣。

Content: Finales should always be a lot of work because it's 3-1/2 minutes.
Content_zh: 結局本來就該花很多工夫，因為它有三分半鐘。

Content: So that might not sound like a lot, but that's actually, that's a really long scene.
Content_zh: 聽起來可能不多，但其實那是一場很長的戲。

Content: It's a really long scene.
Content_zh: 真的很長。

Content: Because if we're really locking the timing down, which is kind of scary.
Content_zh: 因為如果我們真的要把時間鎖死，這有點可怕。

Content: Petty: Yeah, I'm not sure why we'd have to have the music scored before we get the dialogue unless the deadlines are such that.
Content_zh: Petty：對，我不太懂為什麼我們得在拿到對白前就先寫好配樂，除非期限就是那樣。

Content: Crook: I think the music scores, because they're gonna be scoring Grim at the same time, right?
Content_zh: Crook：我想是配樂時程，因為他們會同時替《Grim Fandango》錄配樂，對吧？

Content: Isn't that was this whole idea is?
Content_zh: 這整個想法不就是這樣嗎？

Content: Petty: Oh, is it?
Content_zh: Petty：喔，是嗎？

Content: Crook: I think that's what the deal is because they only want to book the orchestra once.
Content_zh: Crook：我想安排就是這樣，因為他們只想訂一次管弦樂團。

Content: Crook: Isn't the idea they're gonna book the orchestra once and they're gonna do Grim and this at the same time?
Content_zh: Crook：不是說他們要訂一次管弦樂團，然後同時做《Grim Fandango》和這個嗎？

Content: Crook: We were talking about whether we could take it to the next level or not which would be maya animatic.
Content_zh: Crook：我們在討論能不能把它再推到下一個層級，也就是 Maya animatic。

Content: Petty: We certainly can, but there's a ridiculous amount of work right now for all this other stuff.
Content_zh: Petty：當然可以，但現在其他所有東西的工作量已經誇張到不行。

Content: And so it's just a matter of where we're putting; I can't.
Content_zh: 所以問題只是我們要把資源放在哪裡；我沒辦法。

Content: I have the storyboard.
Content_zh: 我手上有分鏡。

Content: I can't really describe all this to Bagel and then have him storyboard it remotely so I have to storyboard it.
Content_zh: 我沒辦法把這些都描述給 Bagel，然後讓他遠端畫分鏡，所以我得自己畫。

Content: No, I mean, it can be done.
Content_zh: 不，我是說，這做得到。

Content: It's just; I think it just gets down to the conversation we always sort of have and don't have an answer for which is date.
Content_zh: 只是，我覺得到最後又會回到我們一直在談、卻沒有答案的那件事，也就是日期。

Content: Petty: Well, it's the only thing that a hard deadline.
Content_zh: Petty：嗯，那是唯一有硬性期限的東西。

Content: Yeah, and then I've been working with Vic on the all timeline stuff too.
Content_zh: 對，然後我也一直在跟 Vic 處理整體時程。

Content: Yeah, I think we're trying to put together like a whole new pass on it and we've talked with most of the leads and everything already about stuff.
Content_zh: 對，我想我們正在試著重新整理一版完整時程，也已經跟大多數負責人談過相關事項。

Content: So probably within the next week or so we'll have a, ooh, bless you.
Content_zh: 所以大概下週左右我們會有一份，喔，保重。

Content: Probably in the next week or so we'll have a new full timeline to share.
Content_zh: 大概下週左右，我們會有新的完整時程可以分享。

Content: I think it's pretty safe to say at this point already that it's not December; like we've already talked about that with pretty much everybody.
Content_zh: 我想現在已經可以很肯定地說不是十二月了；我們其實已經跟幾乎所有人談過這件事。

Content: Schafer: Not me.
Content_zh: Schafer：沒跟我。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we've talked about it multiple times.
Content_zh: Rice：有啊，我們談過好幾次了。

Content: Tim: Yeah, totally ambushed me with that December thing.
Content_zh: Tim：對，他們完全是拿十二月那件事突襲我。

Content: Well, they've all been talking about how they don't feel like we're gonna make December and we probably aren't, but I haven't seen any date.
Content_zh: 他們一直在說覺得我們趕不上十二月，而且我們大概真的趕不上，但我還沒看到任何日期。

Content: You know, it's like it's all hunches.
Content_zh: 你知道，現在都只是直覺。

Content: It's all people's hunches versus hunches, but they're supposed to be doing a waterfall schedule
Content_zh: 全都是大家的直覺對直覺，但他們應該要做一份瀑布式時程，

Content: which is like all the dependencies linked out and that will show us ending a certain month.
Content_zh: 把所有相依工作都串起來，然後那會顯示我們會在哪個月份結束。

Content: And hopefully it's not April.
Content_zh: 希望不是四月。

Content: But it's almost like if you believe it's going to be March then it will be March.
Content_zh: 但這有點像，如果你相信會是三月，它就會變成三月。

Content: And if you're trying to hit December it'll be hopefully January.
Content_zh: 如果你努力瞄準十二月，希望最後會是一月。

Content: But if everyone's just like we're not even trying to make December then we'll slip even worse.
Content_zh: 但如果大家都覺得我們根本不用試著趕十二月，那就會延得更嚴重。

Content: So it's hard to kind of keep a sense of urgency on the project when you feel like; because we're spending our own money on it now
Content_zh: 所以要讓專案保持緊迫感很難，因為現在花的是我們自己的錢，

Content: so it's not like a publisher milestone, like the milestone's due in December and everyone just always takes that seriously.
Content_zh: 不像發行商的里程碑那樣，例如里程碑十二月到期，大家就一定會認真看待。

Content: And when we have something that's an internal milestone or a milestone that only Double Fine is holding you accountable for,
Content_zh: 當它只是內部里程碑，或只有 Double Fine 要你負責的里程碑時，

Content: people are like, well, we've got an extra week and stuff.
Content_zh: 大家就會說，嗯，我們還有多一週之類的。

Content: And that's a hard, a hard thing to instill that sense of like taking a milestone seriously even when it's just us because it's more money.
Content_zh: 要讓大家覺得就算只是我們自己的里程碑，也必須認真看待，這很難，因為那代表更多錢。

Content: Every week that it goes it costs more money.
Content_zh: 每多拖一週，就要花更多錢。

Content: And it's longer the backers will have to wait and it's, you know, the longer that we have to put off other things we want to do.
Content_zh: 贊助者也得等更久，而且我們想做的其他事情也得往後延。

Content: So I still push for getting it done as soon as possible.
Content_zh: 所以我還是會推動大家盡快完成。

Content: Rice: Yeah, there's a lot of work left.
Content_zh: Rice：對，還有很多工作。

Content: It's probably not gonna be December, but we're figuring out when it would be.
Content_zh: 大概不會是十二月，但我們正在釐清會是什麼時候。

Content: It looks like the thing we're thinking, we're hoping for December, is we've got two more weeks on Act 2 stuff,
Content_zh: 目前看起來我們在想的、希望能在十二月完成的是，第二幕還有兩週的工作，

Content: and then the finale stuff we're thinking is like six for like a really solid safe Alpha.
Content_zh: 然後結局部分我們估大概要六週，才能做到很穩、很保險的 Alpha。

Content: And with all the like holiday stuff in before the end of the year and everything, that kind of takes us almost exactly to the end of the year.
Content_zh: 再加上年底前所有假期之類的事情，差不多就會一路做到年底。

Content: Then you have the matter of closing it out is what we're trying to figure out now, so okay.
Content_zh: 接著還有怎麼收尾的問題，那就是我們現在正在釐清的。

Content: Annable: I could've told you like a day back in July it wasn't gonna happen for December.
Content_zh: Annable：早在七月，我隨便挑一天都能告訴你，十二月不可能。

Content: Schafer: I'm gonna wait till I see all the numbers.
Content_zh: Schafer：我要等看到所有數字再說。

Content: Now it's at, well, we're really close to when it should be wrapping up if it's gonna be done in December.
Content_zh: 現在已經接近如果要在十二月完成，理論上該收尾的時間了。

Content: We should have the voice in, and we don't have the final three characters in.
Content_zh: 配音應該已經進來了，但最後三個角色還沒進來。

Content: We're doing that next week.
Content_zh: 我們下週要做那件事。

Content: On Reds I am going down to help with the VO recording which is happening Wednesday, Thursday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday of this week.
Content_zh: Red’s 這邊，我要南下去協助 VO 錄音，時間是這週三、週四、週三、週四、週五。

Content: That's for Vella.
Content_zh: 那是 Vella 的部分。

Content: Brown: I don't feel confident about that at this point.
Content_zh: Brown：到目前為止，我對這件事沒什麼信心。

Content: I'll be honest.
Content_zh: 老實說。

Content: I'm more comfortable saying what I really think because I don't want to say it on camera.
Content_zh: 我比較願意說真正的想法，因為我不想在鏡頭前講。

Content: I'll just be totally blunt.
Content_zh: 我就直說了。

Content: And I'll try to get us out of the studio time that we've purchased for the next four days.
Content_zh: 我會試著取消我們接下來四天已經訂好的錄音室時段。

Content: I'm working on it.
Content_zh: 我正在處理。

Content: Crook: So Tim flew down to record dialogue, Vella in particular, and she had a family emergency.
Content_zh: Crook：所以 Tim 飛下去錄對白，尤其是 Vella 的部分，結果她家裡臨時有急事。

Content: And so that kind of messes everything up.
Content_zh: 所以這有點把一切都打亂了。

Content: I mean, it's not her fault.
Content_zh: 我是說，這不是她的錯。

Content: People have family emergencies.
Content_zh: 人都會遇到家庭急事。

Content: But it definitely kind of went into panic mode a little bit.
Content_zh: 但我們確實有點進入恐慌模式。

Content: Schafer: Do you see I made a tiny gun to shoot myself?
Content_zh: Schafer：你看，我做了一把小槍要拿來自殺嗎？

Content: It looks effective on camera.
Content_zh: 鏡頭上看起來效果不錯。

Content: Crook: There you go. That's better.
Content_zh: Crook：對，就是這樣。好多了。

Content: Open your mouth.
Content_zh: 張開嘴。

Content: It'll be all right, Tim.
Content_zh: 沒事的，Tim。

Content: Hansen: It's all right. We'll figure this out.
Content_zh: Hansen：沒事的，我們會想辦法。

Content: Russell: Vella, for her part of Act 2 is in almost every cut scene so there's very few cut scenes that she's actually not in.
Content_zh: Russell：Vella 在第二幕的部分幾乎每段過場動畫都有她，所以她沒出現的過場動畫非常少。

Content: And if Malena has us up to date as of today, everything that's red hasn't been recorded yet.
Content_zh: 如果 Malena 今天更新的是最新狀態，那所有紅色的部分都還沒錄。

Content: And you can guess who this might be.
Content_zh: 你可以猜猜這可能是誰。

Content: This is Vella.
Content_zh: 這就是 Vella。

Content: This is why we were kind of freaking out.
Content_zh: 這就是為什麼我們有點慌。

Content: That's part of the reason why I made that document, just to figure out, okay, what can we do?
Content_zh: 這也是我做那份文件的原因之一，就是想搞清楚，好，我們能做什麼？

Content: What can we sort of do and may have to reanimate a little bit later?
Content_zh: 有哪些可以先做，但之後可能要稍微重做動畫？

Content: And what's just, we just have to have the voice?
Content_zh: 又有哪些是我們就是非得拿到配音不可？

Content: Otherwise it's just gonna be tough.
Content_zh: 不然真的會很難。

Content: Crook: So anyway we're gonna animate to some scratch.
Content_zh: Crook：總之我們會先用一些臨時音來做動畫。

Content: We're gonna not animate some scenes with Vella.
Content_zh: 有些 Vella 的場景我們會先不做動畫。

Content: It's gonna be a mixture of things.
Content_zh: 會是幾種做法混在一起。

Content: We're gonna be juggling a little bit and then hopefully the first week of December that's what we've been promised.
Content_zh: 我們得稍微調度一下，然後希望十二月第一週能拿到，這是對方承諾的時間。

Content: The first week of December we'll get the recordings done for Vella.
Content_zh: 十二月第一週，我們會完成 Vella 的錄音。

Content: Tim: Which is really frustrating because we're so late with that dialogue and keep being even later.
Content_zh: Tim：這真的很令人沮喪，因為那些對白已經很晚了，還一直往後拖。

Content: It was nothing something we wanted to do.
Content_zh: 這完全不是我們想要的情況。

Content: Annable: Waiting for her, at a certain point it really, really hurts us and I feel like we're already beyond,
Content_zh: Annable：等她這件事，到某個程度真的會非常傷我們，而我覺得我們已經超過了，

Content: like way beyond where we need to be for her recording.
Content_zh: 遠遠超過她錄音應該完成的時間點。

Content: Crook: We'd have to kind of maybe push everybody off of the project onto some other projects because you don't want to be sitting around burning through money, right?
Content_zh: Crook：我們可能得把大家從這個專案暫時調到其他專案，因為你不想讓人坐著乾等、一直燒錢，對吧？

Content: We have to be working on something all the time.
Content_zh: 我們得隨時都有東西可以做。

Content: I mean, it all rides on that December, right?
Content_zh: 我是說，一切都取決於十二月，對吧？

Content: It rides on the recording actually happening in December.
Content_zh: 取決於錄音真的能不能在十二月發生。

Content: Annable: And so if she's not available I think we need to have a second actor cast who is also on hold for the same days
Content_zh: Annable：所以如果她沒空，我覺得我們需要找好第二位演員，也請她保留同樣那些日期，

Content: that we're putting Masasa on hold when she returns.
Content_zh: 也就是我們請 Masasa 回來後保留的日期。

Content: And if Masasa is unavailable then we have to go into the studio with that backup person.
Content_zh: 如果 Masasa 沒空，我們就得和那位備用人選進錄音室。

Content: Schafer: A lot of people on the team are saying we should recast, should recast Vella with an actress that we know we can get and be ready.
Content_zh: Schafer：團隊裡很多人說我們應該重選演員，用一位確定能約到、也能準備好的女演員替 Vella 配音。

Content: I'd be fine with it but now it just bugs me to think of Vella having two voices because there's no such thing as a real sound-alike.
Content_zh: 我可以接受，但一想到 Vella 會有兩種聲音就很煩，因為根本沒有聲音真正一模一樣的人。

Content: You can sound kind of like it, but I think Masasa has a real unique voice and I think players would just know.
Content_zh: 你可以聽起來有點像，但我覺得 Masasa 的聲音很獨特，玩家一定會聽出來。

Content: They would play the first act and then the second act would have a different actress, and it would be that way forever.
Content_zh: 他們玩第一幕，然後第二幕就換成不同演員，而且以後永遠都會是那樣。

Content: It would always be that way.
Content_zh: 它會永遠是那樣。

Content: Annable: It sucks, but I don't want an actor to be responsible for causing so much chaos to our schedule.
Content_zh: Annable：這很糟，但我不希望一位演員變成讓我們時程大亂的原因。

Content: Schafer: So changing Vella feels horrible to me so I'm fighting for keeping the original actress,
Content_zh: Schafer：所以更換 Vella 對我來說感覺很糟，我正在努力保住原本的演員，

Content: and I'm hoping she will come through for me.
Content_zh: 也希望她能幫我把這關過了。

Content: I'm going into L.A. again to see if the magical gopher will come out of its hole and record some dialogue.
Content_zh: 我要再去洛杉磯一趟，看那隻神奇地鼠會不會從洞裡出來錄幾句對白。

Content: We don't know what's gonna happen.
Content_zh: 我們不知道會發生什麼。

Content: But I just go down and we see if she shows up.
Content_zh: 但我就是再跑一趟，看她會不會出現。

Content: Rice: And Tim is reporting right now?
Content_zh: Rice：Tim 現在有回報狀況嗎？

Content: Has anyone heard? Has he sent an email yet?
Content_zh: 有人聽說了嗎？他寄信了嗎？

Content: Crook: We know she showed up.
Content_zh: Crook：我們知道她出現了。

Content: Rice: She did? Okay. She's there. Awesome.
Content_zh: Rice：她出現了？好，她到了。太好了。

Content: Crook: So that's a good step.
Content_zh: Crook：所以這是很好的第一步。

Content: Schafer: Back to the booths.
Content_zh: Schafer：回錄音間吧。

Content: Moyo: A really big explosion.
Content_zh: Moyo：一場超大的爆炸。

Content: I like it when she schemes.
Content_zh: 我喜歡她耍計謀的時候。

Content: Moyo: Hey, President Ugly.
Content_zh: Moyo：嘿，醜總統。

Content: Is that a dig or is that actually his name?
Content_zh: 那是在酸他，還是他真的叫這個名字？

Content: Schafer: It's totally a dig.
Content_zh: Schafer：完全是在酸他。

Content: You're pretty casual, like, hey, what's up?
Content_zh: 你可以很隨性，像是，嘿，怎樣？

Content: Moyo: Everybody thanks you.
Content_zh: Moyo：大家都謝謝你。

Content: [Music] Hansen: So this meeting is to talk about the schedule
Content_zh: [音樂] Hansen：這場會議是要談時程，

Content: for finishing the rest of Act 2.
Content_zh: 也就是完成第二幕剩下部分的時程。

Content: So I've met with Vic and Malena and Greg to talk through the remaining work and to put it all on a timeline to see when the thing's finishing.
Content_zh: 我已經跟 Vic、Malena 和 Greg 開會討論剩下的工作，並把它們排到時程上，看什麼時候能完成。

Content: This should be fairly accurate because you're updated this just recently, right?
Content_zh: 這應該相當準確，因為你們最近才更新過，對吧？

Content: Romero: This is every discussion we've had, every wonderful change to our plans that's come up.
Content_zh: Romero：這包含我們所有討論，以及每個突然冒出來、精彩絕倫的計畫變更。

Content: It's all on there.
Content_zh: 全都在上面。

Content: Schafer: We thought it was going to be April so this is actually good news.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們原本以為會到四月，所以這其實是好消息。

Content: Hansen: It's April to release the game because it takes 3 weeks to manufacture.
Content_zh: Hansen：四月是遊戲發行時間，因為製作實體版需要三週。

Content: But this is when development will be done which is...
Content_zh: 但這是開發會完成的時間，也就是……

Content: Petty: Manufacture?
Content_zh: Petty：製作實體版？

Content: Hansen: Yep, so we're gonna do physical disks.
Content_zh: Hansen：對，所以我們要做實體光碟版。

Content: Petty: Oh, that's right so it has to be simultaneous now, right?
Content_zh: Petty：喔，對，所以現在必須同步發行，對吧？

Content: Hansen: That's part of the deal.
Content_zh: Hansen：那是合約的一部分。

Content: Bailey: So we did a deal with Nordic which is yet to be released, but that's cool.
Content_zh: Bailey：我們跟 Nordic 達成了一筆合作，消息還沒公開，但很棒。

Content: So our deal with Nordic is that they're gonna handle retail distribution of the game in Europe but also here.
Content_zh: 我們跟 Nordic 的合作是，他們會負責這款遊戲在歐洲的零售通路，美國這邊也會。

Content: So they have some relationships with retailers like Best Buy and other really huge big box stores,
Content_zh: 他們跟 Best Buy 之類的零售商，以及其他大型量販通路都有關係，

Content: and it'd be really exciting to see Broken Age show up there.
Content_zh: 能看到《Broken Age》出現在那些地方會很令人興奮。

Content: The way it's actually working out is once we're done with the PC version we can ship it over to them
Content_zh: 實際流程是，只要我們完成 PC 版，就可以把它交給他們，

Content: and they have a very quick, just a few week turnaround which is exciting.
Content_zh: 他們的處理速度很快，只需要幾週，這很令人興奮。

Content: And during that time we're working on the other platforms to get them ready.
Content_zh: 在那段時間，我們會繼續準備其他平台。

Content: So like, you know, when you bought it, whoever's bought it on whatever platform, the hope is everybody can experience the conclusion together.
Content_zh: 所以，不管玩家在哪個平台買了它，我們希望大家都能一起體驗結局。

Content: So that's the goal. We'll see how close we get.
Content_zh: 這就是目標。再看看我們能做到多接近。

Content: Hansen: So as you can see this takes us basically to the end of the year to get the game to Alpha.
Content_zh: Hansen：所以如你所見，讓遊戲進入 Alpha 基本上會做到年底。

Content: And then we would transition on to beta work when we return from the holidays.
Content_zh: 等我們放完假回來，就會轉進 Beta 工作。

Content: And then we have six weeks of bug fixing and polish work at the end.
Content_zh: 最後還有六週修 bug 和打磨。

Content: This is traditionally how long we've needed for all of our projects.
Content_zh: 以往我們所有專案通常都需要這麼久。

Content: There might be some room to pull that in but I do think that the game needs that much time
Content_zh: 也許還有一點壓縮空間，但我確實覺得這款遊戲需要那麼多時間，

Content: in order to address all of the play test feedback, bug fixing and polish work.
Content_zh: 才能處理所有試玩測試回饋、修 bug 和打磨工作。

Content: Schafer: Have you gone over any of this with Justin yet?
Content_zh: Schafer：這些你有跟 Justin 談過了嗎？

Content: Hansen: Only the end date.
Content_zh: Hansen：只有結束日期。

Content: Schafer: What kind of face did he make when you talked to him about the end date?
Content_zh: Schafer：你跟他談結束日期時，他露出什麼表情？

Content: Did he smile? Hansen: No.
Content_zh: 他有笑嗎？Hansen：沒有。

Content: Petty: So somewhere between constipation and Defcon 4. Hansen: Yeah.
Content_zh: Petty：所以介於便祕和 DEFCON 4 之間。Hansen：對。

Content: Schafer: He also only smiles when things are really bad.
Content_zh: Schafer：而且他只有在事情真的很糟時才會笑。

Content: Hansen: It was not a happy face. I'll say that.
Content_zh: Hansen：那不是開心的表情。我只能這麼說。

Content: Schafer: That's the thing.
Content_zh: Schafer：問題就在這裡。

Content: The information, I'm just wondering is what; financially can we go to this date or can't we?
Content_zh: 我想知道的是：從財務上來說，我們能不能撐到這個日期？

Content: Because before we were talking about December and he was kind of like we can't go beyond that because of financial reasons.
Content_zh: 因為之前我們談的是十二月，而他的態度有點像，基於財務理由，我們不能再超過那個時間。

Content: And so that's what I would like to hear from him is what happens if we go to this date.
Content_zh: 所以我想聽他說，如果我們延到這個日期會怎樣。

Content: Hansen: Anything else? Tim, do you have anything else?
Content_zh: Hansen：還有別的嗎？Tim，你還有別的要說嗎？

Content: Schafer: Some inspiring words of wisdom. No.
Content_zh: Schafer：一些鼓舞人心的金玉良言。沒有。

Content: Hey, guys, how's it going?
Content_zh: 嘿，各位，最近怎樣？

Content: I'm getting a strong sense people are trying to give me the message that it's a risk to hit December.
Content_zh: 我強烈感覺大家一直在暗示我：要趕上十二月有風險。

Content: It's gonna be tight to make December.
Content_zh: 要趕上十二月會很吃緊。

Content: That's just me reading between the lines of that meeting.
Content_zh: 這只是我從那場會議裡讀出的弦外之音。

Content: Anyway it's gonna be over soon one way or the other.
Content_zh: 總之不管怎樣，很快就會結束了。

Content: [Music] Peck: You getting this?
Content_zh: [音樂] Peck：你有拍到嗎？

Content: This is good shit.
Content_zh: 這段太讚了。
